raw_consumer_statement:
Barclays has repeatedly refused to investigate and resolve the disputed charges related to XXXX XXXX and XXXX XXXX XXXX. Despite multiple CFPB complaints and direct disputes, Barclays has maintained that the transactions were valid, failing to conduct a reasonable investigation in compliance with federal consumer protection laws. Barclays ' most recent responses, dated XX/XX/year>, XX/XX/year>, XX/XX/year>, and XX/XX/year>, indicate a refusal to acknowledge any new evidence or requests. Additionally : Barclays ' dispute team closed claims due to alleged non-responsiveness, despite clear documentation that the disputes were actively pursued. 
Barclays refused to reverse the charges despite evidence that XXXX XXXX and XXXX XXXX XXXX did not fulfill their contractual obligations. 
Barclays failed to provide any merchant agreements, transaction logs, signed receipts, or proof of service/product delivery for the disputed amounts. 
Barclays ' reporting of these transactions to the CRAs ( despite pending disputes ) constitutes a violation of 15 U.S.C. 1681s-2 ( a ), which prohibits the reporting of inaccurate information.

Legal Violations & Leverageable Laws Fair Credit Billing Act ( FCBA ) - 15 U.S.C. 1666 Requires a creditor to investigate billing disputes and provide evidence supporting the validity of a charge.

Barclays has failed to provide contractual proof, service completion proof, or any other required documentation.

Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ) - 15 U.S.C. 1681s-2 ( b ) Prohibits furnishers of credit information from reporting disputed debts as valid without investigation.

Barclays failed to conduct a reasonable reinvestigation as required by the FCRA.

Truth in Lending Act ( TILA ) - 15 U.S.C. 1601 A credit card issuer must ensure that transactions were authorized and that the merchant fulfilled their obligation before denying a dispute.

Barclays failed to verify service fulfillment before denying your claims.

Consumer Financial Protection Act ( CFPA ) - 12 U.S.C. 5536 ( a ) ( 1 ) ( B ) Prohibits unfair, deceptive, or abusive acts by financial institutions.

Barclays ' refusal to investigate and arbitrary closing of disputes without merit violates CFPA.

Electronic Fund Transfer Act ( EFTA ) - 15 U.S.C. 1693 Requires financial institutions to provide consumers with clear documentation and verification of disputed transactions.

Barclays has not provided adequate documentation proving the legitimacy of the disputed charges.
